reforms.description: The government has initiated a reform of the Fiscal Investment and Loan Programme (FILP) to improve the efficiency of public corporations and reduce their crowding-out effect on the private sector. Additionally, the Council for Science and Technology Policy was established under the Cabinet Office in January 2001 to increase emphasis on basic research and improve the efficiency of public technology spending.
reforms.package_name: Fiscal Investment and Science Policy Reform
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving public technology spending efficiency and emphasizing basic research are expected to enhance innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: Both reforms were enacted and are currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The establishment of a new council and reform of a significant funding program represent major structural changes in the innovation policy landscape.
reforms.source_quote: The reform of the FILP system, which has funded many public corporations, has been in effect since April of this year.

reforms.description: A proposal has been made to allow venture investors to appoint directors to start-up companies, which aims to enhance governance and attract more investment in innovative firms.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing governance in start-ups is expected to attract more investment, thereby fostering innovation and growth in the sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The proposal has been made but not yet legislated or implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, the proposal does not represent a structural change but rather an enhancement of existing governance frameworks.
reforms.source_quote: One proposal that is not controversial is to allow venture investors to appoint directors to a start-up company, which resembles cumulative voting.

reforms.description: A revised patent law was enacted in January 2001, allowing patent attorneys to play a greater role in the trade of intellectual property rights.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating the trade of intellectual property rights is expected to enhance knowledge transfer and commercialization of innovations.
reforms.status_evidence: The revised patent law was enacted and is currently in force.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform improves the existing legal framework for intellectual property but does not represent a fundamental overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: Revised law for patent attorney in force since January 2001.

reforms.description: The establishment of three competing venture security markets in 2001 aims to encourage more start-ups and venture business operations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Creating competitive venture security markets is expected to stimulate investment in start-ups and foster innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The venture security markets were established and are currently operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant for the venture ecosystem, the establishment of markets is an incremental change rather than a structural reform.
reforms.source_quote: Three competing venture security markets now in full operation.

reforms.description: The government introduced a new defined contribution pension system, allowing firms and self-employed individuals to enjoy favorable tax treatment and greater flexibility in managing their pension funds, which is expected to alleviate issues in the corporate pension sector.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The reform is expected to enhance the flexibility and efficiency of pension fund management, potentially leading to improved financial stability and investment in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been successfully put into practice since its introduction.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of a new pension system represents a significant structural change in the corporate pension sector.
reforms.source_quote: To alleviate these problems, drafts of new laws have been submitted to the diet to introduce the defined contribution system and to amend the current defined benefit system.

reforms.description: The government has initiated a shift towards bilateral free trade agreements (FTAs) to complement multilateral trade agreements, with negotiations nearly complete with Singapore, aiming to enhance market access and competition.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The shift towards FTAs is expected to enhance market access and competition, which can stimulate economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has publicly announced the shift towards FTAs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiation of bilateral FTAs represents a significant but narrower change in trade policy.
reforms.source_quote: During the past year the authorities have initiated a shift in policy away from an exclusive reliance on multilateral trade agreements towards bilateral free trade agreements (FTA).
